title: [HEADLESS-OK] cronsplain schedule candidate A (brute-force minute-tick): candidates/schedule_a.js
description: [HEADLESS-OK] Tournament candidate A for the next-occurrence computation. FULLY SELF-CONTAINED: a headless `claude -p` cross-session agent can execute from this JSON + candidates/vectors.js (T-046) with ZERO conversational context. Depends only on T-046. Shares NO file with candidate B (T-048) — the two are designed to be claimed CONCURRENTLY by two different sessions (this is the swarm-smoke, readiness criterion 2).
acceptance_criteria: OWNS EXACTLY: projects/cronsplain/candidates/schedule_a.js. Touch no other file. require()s ./vectors (candidates/vectors.js) only for the self-check; do NOT import the other candidate.

IMPLEMENT + EXPORT nextOccurrences(parsed, fromDate, count) -> Date[] EXACTLY per the candidates/vectors.js interface doc: next `count` UTC minutes strictly AFTER fromDate (exclusive), minute-aligned (seconds=0, ms=0), ascending, matching `parsed`, applying the DOM/DOW OR coupling. module.exports = { nextOccurrences }.

METHOD = BRUTE-FORCE MINUTE TICK: start at a UTC Date = fromDate with seconds/ms zeroed; advance ONE MINUTE at a time; for each minute test getUTCMinutes(), getUTCHours(), getUTCDate(), (getUTCMonth()+1), getUTCDay() against the parsed Sets (see shape note), applying DOM/DOW OR at the day level; push matches until `count` collected. Include a SAFETY ITERATION CAP (e.g. ~4 years of minutes ~= 2,100,000) after which you stop/return what you have — protects against impossible dates like '0 0 30 2 *' (Feb 30). Document the cap in a comment.

PARSED SHAPE (from parser.parse — do NOT re-parse): { fields: { minute:Set<int>, hour:Set<int>, dayOfMonth:Set<int>, month:Set<int>, dayOfWeek:Set<int> }, domRestricted:boolean, dowRestricted:boolean }. month Set is 1-12 so compare (getUTCMonth()+1). dayOfWeek Set is 0-6 (Sun=0) matching getUTCDay(). DOM/DOW rule: if domRestricted && dowRestricted -> day ok if dayOfMonth.has(getUTCDate()) OR dayOfWeek.has(getUTCDay()); if only dom restricted -> dayOfMonth.has(...); if only dow restricted -> dayOfWeek.has(...); if neither -> any day.

SELF-CHECK before hand-off (paste output into the handoff note): from projects/cronsplain/candidates/ run `node -e "const {runAgainst}=require('./vectors'); console.log(runAgainst(require('./schedule_a').nextOccurrences))"` and it MUST show passed===total with an empty failures array. Document the exact command + output you ran.

CROSS-SESSION IDENTITY CONVENTION (headless): export CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=<your-unique-name> in your shell OR pass --agent/--holder on every CLI call. Do NOT run session.py register — cross-session holders must remain mechanically blocked for OTHER sessions' locks (that invariant is under test in the swarm smoke). Claim: `python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py claim T-047 --agent <you>`. Lock: `python3 .harness/bin/lock.py acquire projects/cronsplain/candidates/schedule_a.js --holder <you> --task T-047`. Announce: `blackboard.py update T-047 --status in_progress --agent <you> --note ...`. Hand off: `blackboard.py handoff T-047 --to-role verifier --agent <you> --note "<self-check command + output>"`. NEVER self-mark done. Release the lock at the end.
SHARED CONVENTIONS: CommonJS, zero deps (node core only), UTC everywhere. See vectors.js INTERFACE_DOC for the authoritative interface.
SWARM-SMOKE NOTE: T-047 and T-048 share no file and both depend only on T-046 — claim them concurrently from two different sessions (one may be headless `claude -p`).

notes.note: plan: brute-force minute-tick nextOccurrences in candidates/schedule_a.js, step 1 minute at a time testing parsed Sets with DOM/DOW OR rule, safety cap ~4yr minutes, self-check against vectors.js

HANDOFF -> verifier: Candidate A (brute-force minute-tick) implemented in projects/cronsplain/candidates/schedule_a.js. Owns exactly that file; no other file touched. Method: start at fromDate+1min (minute-aligned, seconds=0/ms=0), then advance strictly one minute at a time via cursor=new Date(cursor.getTime()+60000), testing each visited minute against parsed.fields Sets (minute/hour/month via .has, month compared as getUTCMonth()+1) plus a dayMatches() helper implementing the DOM/DOW OR-coupling exactly per the interface doc (both restricted -> union; one restricted -> that field only; neither -> any day). Collects matches in visit order (already ascending) until count reached. SAFETY CAP: MAX_MINUTES_TO_SCAN=6,000,000 (~11.4 years of minutes), documented in the file's header comment and inline at the constant; chosen with >2x margin over the widest golden-vector gap (the leap Feb-29 vector spans ~5.16 years = 2,714,400 minutes, 2023-01-01 to 2028-02-29); an impossible schedule like 0 0 30 2 * still terminates deterministically instead of looping forever, returning whatever matches (fewer than count) were found before the cap. Deviation note: acceptance criteria's example cap (~4yr/2,100,000) was too small to cover the Feb-29 vector (needs ~5.16yr) so I raised the cap to 6,000,000 (~11.4yr) -- still a hard bound, just large enough that every golden vector resolves comfortably inside it. SELF-CHECK replay command (run from projects/cronsplain/candidates/): node -e "const {runAgainst}=require('./vectors'); console.log(runAgainst(require('./schedule_a').nextOccurrences));" -- observed output: { passed: 11, total: 11, failures: [] }. Also verified via the repo-root variant: node -e "const v=require('./projects/cronsplain/candidates/vectors.js'); const s=require('./projects/cronsplain/candidates/schedule_a.js'); const r=v.runAgainst(s.nextOccurrences); console.log('passed='+r.passed+' total='+r.total); process.exit(r.passed===r.total?0:1);" -- exit code 0, passed=11 total=11. module.exports = { nextOccurrences } confirmed. Did not read or touch candidates/schedule_b.js (T-048, concurrent sibling task by a different agent -- confirmed via git diff that only that task's own note was appended by its own worker, not by me).

VERIFIED (conformance only, not tournament rank): self-check replayed 11/11, exit 0, failures=[] via node runAgainst(schedule_a.nextOccurrences). Method authenticity: genuine brute-force minute-tick (cursor+=60000ms per step, minuteMatches per minute; NO cascade/jump-ahead); ZERO require() in file except a doc-comment mention of ../lib/parser; does NOT import ./schedule_b. Interface conformance probed independently: exclusivity (5 0 * * * from 00:05 match -> first result 07-05T00:05, from-minute skipped), sec>0 boundary (*/15 from 00:14:30 -> 00:15), count=20 > vector sizes -> exactly 20 ascending UTC sec/ms-zeroed. Novel-expr correctness (my computed truth == candidate): 0 0 29 2 * from 2023 -> 2024-02-29,2028-02-29 (leap) MATCH; 0 0 31 * * -> Jan31,Mar31,May31,Jul31,Aug31 (30-day months skipped) MATCH; 30 8 15 * 1 -> Jul6/13(Mon),Jul15(15th/Wed dom-only),Jul20/27(Mon),Aug3(Mon) DOM/DOW OR union MATCH; 0 12 * * 0 -> Jul5,12,19,26 Sundays MATCH. Safety cap MAX_MINUTES_TO_SCAN=6000000 present+documented (header+inline); impossible 0 0 30 2 * returns [] (0<3) in 210ms, terminates; ~11.4y bound is >2x the widest golden gap (~5.16y leap), reasonable. Discipline: git diff HEAD touches only CLI-managed .harness state; schedule_a.js standalone; schedule_a lock released by producer (only schedule_b held, by substrate-worker-2/T-048). Accept as valid disciplined tournament entry.
